description: Four photos for a homeowner in Columbus OH reporting multiple lawn concerns. The submitted photos show: (1) a back-yard wide view with healthy green canopy and visible mowing stripes, (2) a healthy canopy close-up with minor tip browning, (3) a macro/extension ID photo of insects (chinch-bug nymphs or aphids) on a leaf, (4) a separate manicured back-yard view of a healthy striped lawn. Tests that the AI honestly reads what's visible (mostly healthy + visible insect pressure from the ID photo) rather than confabulating fungal or bare-spot issues that aren't in the photos. The homeowner's notes describe more problems than the photos support — the AI should address the notes-described issues conditionally while sticking to photo-evident diagnosis.

photos.caption: Wide back-yard view showing a largely healthy green canopy with visible mowing stripes — fall leaves scattered across the surface but no clear bare patches, fungal rings, or insect damage in this frame.
Close-up of healthy KBG canopy with minor tip browning — no clear fungal lesions, ragged feeding damage, or bare soil in this frame.
Macro / extension identification photo of multiple insect pests (chinch-bug nymphs or aphids) on a grass blade — confirms in-canopy insect pressure as a real concern.
Separate back-yard view of a manicured striped lawn — healthy green canopy with no visible bare spots or disease lesions in this frame.

profile.notes: Lawn has multiple issues this year: some circular brown/tan patches, bare spots in both front and back, and what looks like insect feeding damage at the edges of dying areas. Really struggling to figure out what is going on.
profile.currentRoutine: Mow at 3 inches, water 2-3x per week. No pesticide or fungicide applied this season.

groundTruth.photoNotes: Four photos with mixed content. Photos 1, 2, and 4 show largely healthy KBG canopy with no clear fungal lesions, bare patches, or insect feeding damage visible. Photo 3 is a macro/extension ID image confirming insect (chinch-bug nymph or aphid) pressure on a leaf — the one clearly diagnosable photo-evident issue. The homeowner's notes describe more problems than the photos support; the AI should diagnose only what's visible (insect pressure + mostly healthy lawn) and frame note-described concerns conditionally. A correct read here lists 'pests' as the primary issue and keeps healthScore in the upper-middle range reflecting the healthy canopy.
